name: Divide and Conquer
def: Splits a problem into independent subproblems, solves recursively, then combines results.
problem: Break a large problem into smaller, independent instances that are cheaper to solve and merge.
mechanism: Recursively partition until a base case, solve directly, and combine. Runtime follows the Master Theorem T(n)=aT(n/b)+f(n).
limits: Poor fit when subproblems overlap (use DP)
whenNot: Overlapping subproblems (DP) or trivially sequential work
Tiny inputs where recursion overhead dominates
myths: "Always parallel-friendly" — only when subproblems are truly independent

complexity.Note: Per Master Theorem, e.g. O(n log n) for mergesort
